subtopics.title: What's in the Box
subtopics.content:
#### Player Components
- **Player boards** — 1 per player; each is a 4x4 grid (16 spaces).
- **Building cards** — 7 types of buildings, each showing a resource pattern and scoring rule.
- **Monument cards** — Unique buildings; each player selects 1 at game start.

#### Resources
- **Resource cubes** — 5 types: Wood (brown), Wheat (yellow), Brick (red), Glass (blue), Stone (gray).
- **Quantity** — Enough cubes for all players to place simultaneously.

#### Building Miniatures
- **Wooden buildings** — Small colored wooden pieces representing constructed buildings.
- **Types** — Cottage (blue), Chapel (yellow), Tavern (red), Theater (orange), Factory (gray), Trading Post (green), Well (purple), and others depending on card selection.

#### Other
- **Master Builder hammer** — Token passed to indicate the Master Builder.
- **Resource cards** — 5 cards (one per resource type) for the Master Builder to choose from.

subtopics.title: Board Layout
subtopics.content:
1. **Distribute boards** — Give each player a 4x4 grid board.
2. **Select building cards** — Choose 1 card per building type (7 total). Each type offers multiple options for variety.
3. **Display buildings** — Place selected building cards face up where all players can see them.
4. **Monument selection** — Deal 2 monument cards to each player; each player secretly chooses 1 and returns the other.
5. **Resource supply** — Place all resource cubes in accessible shared piles.
6. **Building supply** — Place building miniatures in shared supply.
7. **Master Builder token** — Give the hammer to a starting player.

subtopics.title: Player Setup
subtopics.content:
#### Each Player Begins With
- **Empty 4x4 grid** — All 16 spaces are empty.
- **1 monument card** — Kept secret until built.
- **No resources** — Resources are placed during the game.

#### Solo Variant
- **Solo mode** — Draw resource cards from a shuffled deck instead of using a Master Builder.
- **3 face-up cards** — On your turn, choose 1 of 3 revealed resource cards; refill.

--- 2-6 Players ---
- **Standard Master Builder** — One player names a resource; all must place it.

subtopics.title: Starting the Game
subtopics.content:
1. **Master Builder goes first** — The Master Builder names a resource type.
2. **All players place** — Every player (including the Master Builder) takes that resource cube and places it on any empty space on their grid.
3. **Optional construction** — After placing, any player may construct a building if the correct pattern is present on their board.
4. **Pass the hammer** — After all players have placed and optionally built, pass the Master Builder token clockwise.
5. **Continue** — The new Master Builder names the next resource.

subtopics.title: Turn Structure
subtopics.content:
#### Each Turn
1. **Master Builder names a resource** — Choose one of the 5 resource types.
2. **All players simultaneously place** — Take a cube of that resource and place it on any empty space on your 4x4 grid.
3. **Optional construction** — After placement, any player may construct one or more buildings.
4. **Pass Master Builder** — Rotate the hammer clockwise.

#### Construction
- **Pattern matching** — If resources on your grid match the exact pattern shown on a building card (in any orientation/rotation), you may construct.
- **Remove resources** — Remove the resource cubes used in the pattern.
- **Place building** — Put the corresponding building miniature on one of the spaces that was part of the pattern.
- **Multiple builds** — You may construct multiple buildings in one turn if multiple patterns are complete.
- **Pattern flexibility** — Patterns can be rotated but not mirrored.

subtopics.title: Actions & Options
subtopics.content:
#### Master Builder Choice
- **Resource selection** — Name any of the 5 resource types.
- **Strategic naming** — Choose resources that help you while hindering opponents.
- **Information** — You can see opponents' boards; use this to name unhelpful resources for them.

#### Placement Rules
- **Any empty space** — Resource cubes can go on any unoccupied grid space.
- **No stacking** — Only 1 cube or 1 building per space.
- **Mandatory placement** — You must place the named resource even if it hurts your plans.

#### Building Rules
- **Pattern match** — Resources must match the building card's pattern exactly.
- **Orientation** — Patterns may be rotated 90, 180, or 270 degrees.
- **Building placement** — The building miniature goes on any one of the spaces that held the pattern's resources.
- **Multiple copies** — You may build the same building type multiple times.
- **Monuments** — Your unique monument works like other buildings but only you can build it.

#### Building Types & Scoring
- **Cottage** — Scores if fed (adjacent to a completed Farm-type building).
- **Chapel** — Scores incrementally based on number of fed Cottages when built.
- **Tavern** — Scores in sets (1/2/3/4/5 Taverns = 2/5/9/14/20 points).
- **Theater** — Scores based on number of unique building types in the same row and column.
- **Factory** — Allows naming your own resource once per turn when placed.
- **Other buildings** — Vary by selected card; each has unique scoring conditions.

subtopics.title: Special Mechanics
subtopics.content:
#### Factory Building
- **Personal resource** — When you have a Factory, on each turn after the Master Builder names a resource, you may additionally place a resource of your own choice.
- **Placement order** — Place the Master Builder's resource first, then your Factory resource.
- **Multiple Factories** — Multiple Factories do not stack (still only 1 extra resource per turn).

#### Monument
- **Unique building** — Each player has a different monument with a unique scoring rule.
- **Secret until built** — Keep your monument card hidden until you construct it.
- **One per game** — You can only build your monument once.
- **Powerful scoring** — Monuments typically score high points with specific conditions.

#### Grid Management
- **Space is precious** — The 4x4 grid has only 16 spaces; every placement matters.
- **Unused resources** — Resources stuck on the grid that cannot form patterns become dead spaces.
- **Empty space penalty** — Each empty space at game end is -1 VP.
- **Impossible positions** — Sometimes the Master Builder forces resources that clog your grid.

#### Town Hall Variant
- **Open resource selection** — Instead of a Master Builder, draw resource cards from a shared deck.
- **All players choose** — Each player picks from available resources, adding variety.

subtopics.title: Endgame & Scoring
subtopics.content:
#### End Trigger
- **Grid full** — The game ends for a player when their 4x4 grid is completely full (all 16 spaces occupied by resources or buildings).
- **Other players continue** — Players with open spaces keep playing.
- **Game ends** — When all players' grids are full, proceed to scoring.

#### Scoring
- **Building VP** — Each building scores based on its card's rules.
- **Cottage VP** — Each fed cottage scores its printed value.
- **Chapel VP** — Scores based on fed cottages at time of construction.
- **Tavern VP** — Set collection: 1/2/3/4/5 taverns = 2/5/9/14/20 points.
- **Theater VP** — Points for unique building types in same row and column.
- **Factory VP** — Often scores a flat amount.
- **Monument VP** — Scores based on monument card's unique condition.
- **Other buildings** — Score per their selected card.
- **Empty space penalty** — Each empty grid space at game end = -1 VP.

#### Winner
- **Most VP wins** — Add all building scores, subtract empty space penalties.
- **Tiebreaker** — Player with fewer empty spaces wins; if still tied, shared victory.

subtopics.title: Opening Priorities
subtopics.content:
- **Plan your layout** — Mentally map where buildings will go before placing resources; the grid fills fast.
- **Build Factories early** — Getting an extra resource per turn is a significant advantage.
- **Start in corners** — Build patterns from edges and corners to avoid trapping yourself.
- **Know your monument** — Design your grid around your monument's scoring condition.
- **Watch the Master Builder** — Anticipate what resources opponents will name and pre-position accordingly.

subtopics.title: Common Mistakes
subtopics.content:
- **Ignoring grid space** — Placing resources without a plan leads to dead cubes filling your grid.
- **Chasing too many building types** — Spreading resources thin prevents completing any pattern.
- **Forgetting the empty penalty** — Dead spaces cost -1 each; a grid with 4 empty spaces loses 4 points.
- **Ignoring opponents' boards** — As Master Builder, naming resources that help opponents wastes your advantage.
- **Building in the center** — Central buildings block adjacency paths and restrict future placement options.
- **Neglecting Cottages** — Cottages are a reliable point engine; ignoring them misses easy VP.

subtopics.title: Key Decisions
subtopics.content:
- **Master Builder resource naming** — Choose resources that advance your pattern while stalling opponents.
- **When to build** — Whether to complete a pattern now or wait for a better building placement position.
- **Building placement within pattern** — Which space in the pattern to place the building affects future adjacency scoring.
- **Monument timing** — Build early for utility or late when scoring conditions are optimized.
- **Accept dead spaces vs force builds** — Sometimes building a low-value building is better than leaving resources stranded.
